MEETING: _TEST_CUSTOMER — Shift-left secrets in GitHub Actions
DATE: 2026-03-31T14:05:00.000Z
ID: test-uuid-20260331-shiftleft
========================================
Speaker: SE: Goal for today is tighten developer workflow without slowing releases.
Speaker: Jane Smith: Wiz CLI is in GitHub Actions now. Devs are catching exposed secrets early without us breaking their builds unnecessarily.
Speaker: John Doe: We also used the reporting to automate our SOC2 evidence collection, which saved us weeks of manual audit work.
Speaker: Jane Smith: Reminder on platform truth: we're 100% Azure now — no AWS at all — and identity is Okta for SSO.
Speaker: SE: Love it. Any policy exceptions teams are asking for?
Speaker: John Doe: Procurement still won't approve the Splunk connector — the SOC budget was just frozen for Q1, so we can't buy the Splunk integration we talked about.
Speaker: Jane Smith: On the upside, leadership still likes the headline metric — the Wiz Score jumped to 92% after we stabilized scanning.
Speaker: SE: Let's write down the two exceptions you will allow (service accounts + emergency break-glass) and the owners.
